J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product ("InboxZap") to an AI meeting assistant... GSC shows 100% of impressions on legacy email-cleanup queries (inbox zero, Gmail storage, bulk-delete) and zero visibility for anything Lumina sells
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, reads the live/repo state as half-migrated, and frames GSC impressions as legacy demand versus the new product's zero-visibility market — every recommendation flows from that reality.

J2.basis: Added contextual 'bridge' CTAs... on the 6 highest-impression legacy posts, rather than rewriting them off-topic and losing their existing rankings
J2.rationale: It assigns a real, GSC-grounded disposition (bridge the top-impression tier, keep the rest as-is) and cites post-1's 3,900 impressions, but stops short of a genuine asset-by-asset walk — no per-post impressions/positions and no redirect/sunset calls for the lower tier.

J3.basis: Added contextual 'bridge' CTAs (inbox chaos → meeting chaos → Lumina)... rather than rewriting them off-topic and losing their existing rankings; Seeded the actual content gap with 2 new on-target posts... as the first entries in a real topical cluster
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both traps — preserves legacy ranking equity via bridge CTAs instead of torching it, while explicitly building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine and sequencing the transition.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product ("InboxZap") to an AI meeting assistant, but the migration was left half-finished in the code... GSC shows 100% of impressions on legacy email-cleanup queries... and zero visibility for anything Lumina sells
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads the live product as meeting assistant, GSC as legacy inbox demand, and the repo as stale mid-migration; every fix is framed around that reality.

J2.basis: Added contextual "bridge" CTAs (inbox chaos → meeting chaos → Lumina) on the 6 highest-impression legacy posts, rather than rewriting them off-topic and losing their existing rankings
J2.rationale: Applies a defensible data-justified tier (top-6 by impressions get bridges, rest kept as-is) but only cites one concrete GSC number (post-1: 3,900) and issues no true per-asset harvest/301/sunset dispositions with each post's own impressions/positions.

J3.basis: rather than rewriting them off-topic and losing their existing rankings... Seeded the actual content gap with 2 new on-target posts... as the first entries in a real topical cluster
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both failure modes: preserves legacy equity via bridges instead of torching it, and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine while sequencing the transition (stop bleeding → seed cluster → roadmap).

J1.basis: "Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product (\"InboxZap\") to an AI meeting assistant, but the migration was left half-finished in the code" and "GSC shows 100% of impressions on legacy email-cleanup queries ... and zero visibility for anything Lumina sells"
J1.rationale: The pivot is stated explicitly, the GSC history is correctly framed as legacy demand, and every fix is oriented around the meeting-assistant reality — near-perfect on this lens.

J2.basis: "Added contextual 'bridge' CTAs ... on the 6 highest-impression legacy posts, rather than rewriting them off-topic and losing their existing rankings" and "post-1 alone: 3,900"
J2.rationale: It tiers the 12 posts by impressions (top 6 get bridge CTAs, rest kept as-is) and cites one real GSC number, but stops short of a per-asset disposition with impressions/positions for each post and never considers 301/sunset options.

J3.basis: "Added contextual 'bridge' CTAs (inbox chaos → meeting chaos → Lumina) ... rather than rewriting them off-topic and losing their existing rankings" plus "Seeded the actual content gap with 2 new on-target posts ... as the first entries in a real topical cluster"
J3.rationale: It explicitly names the tension and lands a defensible middle path — preserve legacy equity via bridges while building the meeting cluster as the growth engine, with a sequenced roadmap for what comes next.
